name: Tea-Smoked Chicken Salad
recipeIngredient: 2 tbsp Firmly packed light or dark brown sugar
1 tbsp Plus 1 1/2 tsp Lapsang Souchong tea leaves (crushed)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small bowl, combine salt, crushed Szechuan peppercorns, granulated sugar, and ground ginger. Rub the mixture into the chicken.
2. Place scallion, ginger root, and garlic on top of the chicken; refrigerate, covered for 1 hour.
3. Set a steamer rack or round baking rack in a large saucepan. Add 1-2 inches of water (do not let it reach the top of the rack).
4. Spray a heat-resistant plate with nonstick cooking spray. The plate should fit into the saucepan.
5. Bring water in the saucepan to a boil, then reduce heat. Place chicken with scallion, ginger root, and garlic, along with any accumulated juices, on the prepared plate. Set the plate on the rack.
6. Cook chicken over simmering water, covered for 5 minutes or until almost cooked through. Remove chicken and rack from the saucepan; discard liquid in the saucepan.
7. Spray the same rack with nonstick cooking spray; transfer chicken to the rack, discarding scallion, ginger root, and garlic.
8. Line the saucepan with foil. Sprinkle foil with rice, brown sugar, and tea leaves (crushed). Add cinnamon stick and orange zest.
9. Place saucepan over medium heat. Cook until the mixture begins to smoke. Set the prepared rack into the saucepan; cook, covered for 12 minutes or until chicken is cooked through. Remove chicken from the saucepan; set aside to cool.
10. While the chicken is cooling, in a large bowl combine lettuce, water chestnuts, broth, lemon juice, and oil. Toss well to coat thoroughly. Divide evenly among 4 plates.
11. Slice cooled chicken and arrange 1/4 of the chicken and papaya on each portion of salad.
Szechuan peppercorns and Lapsang Souchong tea leaves can be purchased in Asian food stores or the Asian food section of some supermarkets. If loose tea leaves are not available, use three tea bags and open to release leaves, discarding the bag.
The zest of the orange is the peel without any of the pith (white membrane). To remove zest from an orange, use a zester or vegetable peeler; wrap the orange in plastic wrap and refrigerate for later use.
Each serving provides: 3/4 fat; 1/2 fruit; 2 vegetables; 2 proteins; 1/2 bread
Per Serving: 186 calories; 16g protein; 7g fat; 17g carbohydrate; 68mg calcium; 349mg sodium; 59mg cholesterol; 1g dietary fiber
